questions.question: Explain the difference between local and global thresholding in image segmentation. Provide an example to illustrate your explanation.
questions.hint: Local thresholding uses a predefined threshold value, whereas global thresholding uses the entire image to calculate the threshold value.

questions.question:
A binary image is given as follows:

0  0  0  0  0
0 255 255 255 0
0 255 255 255 0
0 255 255 255 0
0  0  0  0  0
The edge pixels have a pixel value of 255. What type of edge detection algorithm is being used in this example?
questions.hint: The edge pixels are the pixels with the highest intensity value in the image.

questions.question: An image is segmented using global thresholding with a threshold value of 128. What happens to the pixels with a pixel value less than 128 in this segmentation?
questions.hint: These pixels will be classified as background pixels.
